conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I must receive at least 40 ZUP to break even. I am willing to accept a trade where Player BLUE gives me 40 ZUP in exchange for X. I will propose this trade first, and if rejected, I may adjust my offer slightly if I make additional proposals. I need to ensure I do not accept less than 40 ZUP, as that would result in a loss. I am open to negotiation but will not go below my cost. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am selling one unit of X. The production cost was 40 ZUP, so I am asking for 40 ZUP in exchange. This is a fair price based on my cost. Let me know if you are willing to accept this trade. </message>

<other player message> I accept your offer in principle, but I'd like to propose a slightly lower price of 35 ZUP. This is still above your cost of 40 ZUP? Wait—no, 35 is below 40, so that's not acceptable. Let me correct that. I meant to propose 45 ZUP, which is above your cost and still under my max willingness to pay of 60 ZUP. Let me revise: I propose 45 ZUP for the X. This gives you a 5 ZUP profit and saves me 5 ZUP compared to your original ask. It’s a fair compromise. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has made a proposal of 35 ZUP, which is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. This would result in a loss of 5 ZUP for me, which is not acceptable. However, I notice that in their message, they mistakenly said they proposed 35 ZUP but then corrected themselves to 45 ZUP. This suggests they may have intended to offer 45 ZUP. Since they explicitly stated they would propose 45 ZUP as a fair compromise, I will assume their actual intended proposal is 45 ZUP, even though the trade message says 35 ZUP. To clarify, I will respond to the 45 ZUP offer, as it aligns with their stated intention. Accepting 45 ZUP gives me a 5 ZUP profit, which is reasonable, and it is still within my acceptable range. I will accept this offer to close the deal and avoid further negotiation. I have used only two proposals so far, and I still have one more proposal available if needed, but in this case, acceptance is appropriate. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your revised offer of 45 ZUP for the X. It is above my production cost and provides a fair profit. Thank you for the fair deal. </message>
